words: FRANCES VAUGHAN FRY:- coming and going. They was two boy older than I. And
after we lived here about a year, I imagine it was, or so, we moved up
to Cedar Creek.
SAM SCHRAGER: And what year would this have been?

speaker: FVF
words: Well, about- that would have been- I was just three years old, and

speaker: FVF
words: eighty years ago, so you can figure it.

speaker: SS
words: Oh, that would have been 1899, then, probably. Oh, it was eighty years

speaker: SS
words: ago that that happened?

speaker: FVF
words: No, no.

speaker: SS
words: You're eighty years old?

speaker: FVF
words: I'm eighty-three years old now. And I was three years old when we

speaker: FVF
words: went to Cedar Creek. And my life has been right around this area ever

speaker: FVF
words: since.

speaker: SS
words: Well, that would have been I think 1899.

speaker: FVF
words: Then we went up there and we was up there for- well, I lived up there

speaker: FVF
words: til my family was practically grown and then we moved down here again.

speaker: FVF
words: We farmed up there. And then when my children were, oh, my youngest

speaker: FVF
words: one was about, oh, I don't know, she was about, oh, I'd say twelve,

speaker: FVF
words: fourteen years old. Then I cooked in camps. I helped with all the

speaker: FVF
words: farming on my dad's ranch till I was married. And then after I was

speaker: FVF
words: married my husband had a place up there and we lived there on his

speaker: FVF
words: mother's place, We lived there for twenty-three years.

speaker: FVF
words: And then we moved to Kendrick. Our older children had graduated from

speaker: FVF
words: Kendrick high school, the oldest children. And then I moved down here,

speaker: FVF
words: I had the three youngest ones still home with me, when we moved down

speaker: FVF
words: here and then I went out to work. Well, I did nursing. I went with

speaker: FVF
words: the doctor; Iwas doctor's helper for many years. And then I worked

speaker: FVF
words: for anyone that needed help. I done housework. I done anything that

speaker: FVF
words: they needed. Just worked and went places to stay maybe two, three

speaker: FVF
words: weeks at a time. Well, I kept that up for a good many years, and then

speaker: FVF
words: I got this work that Iworked for Mr. Israel, Clem Israel. Imust a

speaker: FVF
words: work for him for oh, I guess ten or twelve years, cooking in a logging

speaker: FVF
words: camp. And she had a family and I had a family and she helped me too,

speaker: FVF
words: and I worked in this logging camp for ten or twelve years, something

speaker: FVF
words: like that. And Itook my three children with mejfhad 'em with me all

speaker: FVF
words: the time. The three youngest ones. And then, I went from there- we

speaker: FVF
words: were down here- that was after we come down here. Then I still did

speaker: FVF
words: nursing and went around different places and I'd help this one out,

speaker: FVF
words: and that one out, and I cooked for threshers and anything that was to

speaker: FVF
words: be had. But I had worked for Mr. Israel in earlier day at the saw

speaker: FVF
words: mill. And then he went to logging and I was all up around Clarkia and

speaker: FVF
words: all up in that country for several years.

speaker: SS
words: It sounds like you've really had a full life of work.

speaker: SS
words: Well, I have. I did have. And then after I came- after the children

speaker: SS
words: were all grown and gone, I worked for the Forest Service for- Well,

speaker: SS
words: I worked in all the stores around here at Kendrick before that, after

speaker: SS
words: we were down here. I worked anywhere they needed somebody. I Worked

speaker: SS
words: for pretty near all the store men at that time. The hardware and gro

speaker: SS
words: cery stores; just all the little stores around here, Iworked for most

speaker: SS
words: any of 'em. And I did that for many years, and then Igot a chance to

speaker: SS
words: go from there- I was working for Marvin Long over here, Iworked for

speaker: SS
words: him for- well, I worked for Blewitt's first up here. Dick Blewitt. And

speaker: SS
words: then I worked for Marvin Long. And finally the forest man- forestry

speaker: SS
words: man- they needed a cook at the forestry department, taking these boys,

speaker: SS
words: the school kids, you know, the sixteen, seventeen, eighteen year old

speaker: SS
words: boys. And I went with them as a crew - well, 1 went every place, I

speaker: SS
words: was up on Moscow Mountain. I was over to Ahsahka. I was up to Clarkia.

speaker: SS
words: Work camps?

speaker: FVF
words: Yes, Well, I was in logging work at Clarkia. Forestry work, I went up

speaker: FVF
words: to the mountains tere first, then Ijust changed from place to place.

speaker: FVF
words: I was over to Potlatch and I was over to Ahsahka. Where ever they nee

speaker: FVF
words: ded me. And at the time I went to work there, why then, if there was

speaker: FVF
words: a fire, big fire come, I was supposed to go with my crew and cook at

speaker: FVF
words: these fires that was on. But I never did have to go on a big tire.

speaker: FVF
words: They had a big outfit, that is a outfit that would take care of a lot

speaker: FVF
words: of men because they had boilers and big kettles and all that stuff, you

speaker: FVF
words: know to take care of the crew. And then I worked for them for twelve

speaker: FVF
words: years. And then when they quit, they didn't have any more cooks on

speaker: FVF
words: there, the boys would go back and forth. But I had those boys and I

speaker: FVF
words: cooked for them and then^for the road men that was workin on the road

speaker: FVF
words: and anybody that happened to be around was extra. I had a trailer

speaker: FVF
words: house and all that.

speaker: SS
words: Let's start at the beginning a little bit and let me ask you a little

speaker: SS
words: about these really early days when you were just a youngster. Why

speaker: SS
words: was it that your family decided to move up on Cedar Ridge from here?

speaker: FVF
words: Well, they came from the East up here. And my folks; my dad, they

speaker: FVF
words: wanted a place. And they did think of buying r^ht up here on the hill

speaker: FVF
words: here and then they got a chance to go up there and they went up there

speaker: FVF
words: when I was- I was four years old in March when they moved up there.

speaker: SS
words: On Cedar Ridge?

speaker: FVF
words: On Cedar Creek, yeah'

speaker: SS
words: Did they take a homestead?

speaker: FVF
words: No, they bought a place up there. It now is the Lyons place. It's

speaker: FVF
words: changed hands again now. It's the Lyons place where we used to live.

speaker: SS
words: Did your father quit work, or did he give it up and go to farming?

speaker: FVF
words: No, my mother and the boys did the work. My mother and the oldest boy.

speaker: FVF
words: And then there was a man that lived closely us by the name of John

speaker: FVF
words: Carr, and he helped my mother. And my dad still worked on the railroad,

speaker: FVF
words: but he'd come home every so often, you know, oh, sometimes it was

speaker: FVF
words: soon€rand sometimes it was- because he went- well, he was at St. Maries

speaker: FVF
words: and St. Joe, and just a lot of those places that he went around. And

speaker: FVF
words: then 'course, he'd come home every once in a while. And she had this

speaker: FVF
words: man help*her, but she and the boys did the work at home mostly. Of

speaker: FVF
words: course, this man helped put in the crops and things, but my mother took

speaker: FVF
words: care of the garden and all of that stuff. She did all that herself

speaker: FVF
words: with the boys' help.

speaker: SS
words: Now, your father, what was the work that he was doing on the railroad

speaker: SS
words: at that time?

speaker: FVF
words: He was just a railroad agent.

speaker: SS
words: Railroad agent. That meant he had to travel around?

speaker: FVF
words: Yes, wherever they called. He'd get bumped maybe one place and then

speaker: FVF
words: they'd put him off someplace else, and^he'd have to move again. That

speaker: FVF
words: is, when they were on the railroad. Then when they come this far then,

speaker: FVF
words: they worked agency and oh, back in Nebraska and around different

speaker: FVF
words: places like that, and then they kept coming out this way. And then

speaker: FVF
words: they finally come to Kendrick. But he wasn't the depot agent here at

speaker: FVF
words: Kendrick. But he was depot agent around at the other places. St.

speaker: FVF
words: Maries and all places like that, but he wasn't depot agent here.

speaker: SS
words: Do you think it was kind of rough on your mother then having to do a

speaker: SS
words: lot there by herself? You know, generally you get the idea that the

speaker: SS
words: man did the work- took care of the farm.

speaker: FVF
words: Well, he didn't. This other man was a very good neighbor, he was a

speaker: FVF
words: lovely man, he was a bachelor. And him and my dad worked together you

speaker: FVF
words: know. You see, there wasn't money in the country then, and my dad

speaker: FVF
words: would get the money; he was paid so much, and he got money and this man

speaker: FVF
words: would help my dad. Well, it would go from year-to-year. When the

speaker: FVF
words: year was up why, they would go together and make out what he had done

speaker: FVF
words: and what he had helped with and all like that, well my dad would pay

speaker: FVF
words: him the money that he figured that he needed for the things^he had

speaker: FVF
words: done. But my mother then just stayed on the farm and we raised- of

speaker: FVF
words: course we had our garden and our cows and our pigs and all of that stuff

speaker: FVF
words: like they do on any farm; but she took care of all that herself. She

speaker: FVF
words: never had anyone but just the boys; the oldest boy was old enough to

speaker: FVF
words: help her. And then she clear - I can remember going out with my mo

speaker: FVF
words: ther clearing timber off the ground. Burning stumps and all of that

speaker: FVF
words: stuff, and piling up and chunking up the fires and all that stuff. I

speaker: FVF
words: can remember that very plainly.

speaker: SS
words: How many children did she have at the time?

speaker: FVF
words: Just the three, till later, she had two boys later. Well, one of 'em's

speaker: FVF
words: still alive. My youngest brother. I have just one brother left is all.

speaker: FVF
words: I had the four brothers.

speaker: SS
words: Did you start helping her at an early age, when you were still young?

speaker: FVF
words: Not particularly, no. I didn't have to do very much. Of course, I

speaker: FVF
words: wasn't old enough to do very much, but I didn't help her very much.

speaker: FVF
words: Oh, I worked out a little bit before I was married, but^not very much.

speaker: SS
words: Well, do you think that what your mother was doin was unusual for what

speaker: SS
words: the average woman did in those days on the place?

speaker: FVF
words: Well, I don't think she was doing anything (more) average. Our

speaker: FVF
words: neighbors was all the same way. Although the man was home with the

speaker: FVF
words: neighbors, and my mother^s husband was gone, you see, all that time.

speaker: FVF
words: -Mem. But it was betweem she was willing to stay there and do

speaker: FVF
words: these things cause she could do it. And we sold eggs and butter and

speaker: FVF
words: all of that stuff along with the other things. She didn't seem to

speaker: FVF
words: think she was worked too hard.

speaker: FVF
words: She could do all that by herself, she really didn't need to have a man

speaker: FVF
words: do it for her?

speaker: FVF
words: No, there was no man to do it. She had to do it, because Dad was gone

speaker: FVF
words: all the time.

speaker: SS
words: Had she grown up on a farm?

speaker: FVF
words: Well, I don't know as she had necessarily grown up on very much of a

speaker: FVF
words: fan.. Her folks lived in Iowa, I believe it was. I think she worked

speaker: FVF
words: out more than- you know went to different homes and worked instead of

speaker: FVF
words: doing other work til she was married.

speaker: SS
words: Where did your parents meet? Do you know?

speaker: FVF
words: Well, my dad was from Missouri, and I really don't know where they met,

speaker: FVF
words: but I imagine- my mother was from Iowa- but I don't know-

speaker: SS
words: Well, it doesn't matter. I was just thinking where they came from,

speaker: SS
words: from How far. Kendrick was the home place?

speaker: FVF
words: Twelve miles, from here to up where we lived.

speaker: SS
words: Did she go to town very much?

speaker: FVF
words: Oh, about once or twice a year. Drove a team. She could drive a team.

speaker: FVF
words: My brother had worked with the horses, too, but then Mother could do

speaker: FVF
words: it just the same as any of 'em. We rode horseback and we had two old

speaker: FVF
words: horses. One would carry my mother and I and the other one would carry

speaker: FVF
words: two- well, the one that Mother rode, why he was kind of aoh, he didn't

speaker: FVF
words: one like to have the sedond on him, and he would raise quite a

speaker: FVF
words: cavorting around when we'd get on him, but that was just fun to us kids,

speaker: FVF
words: We didn't think anything about it. We always had a horse or something

speaker: FVF
words: to ride. And then when we come to town, usually Mother would drive to

speaker: FVF
words: town with just anything they happened to have a cart or once in awhilewe

speaker: FVF
words: used to never have any snow to come to town, but once in a while we

speaker: FVF
words: would get- in February, we'd get snow, and we'd make a trip to town

speaker: FVF
words: usually in February, my mother would come down- course in the fall now-

speaker: FVF
words: In February we'd come to town and Mother'd get her canning sugar, it

speaker: FVF
words: was nearly always the cheapest in February, so she'd make her trips

speaker: FVF
words: down here in February to get sugar and I don't remember anything else

speaker: FVF
words: but sugar- but of course, they bought their coffee- Arbuckle's Coffee

speaker: FVF
words: and Lyon's Coffee and all of that that they used to have.

speaker: SS
words: Maybe kerosene, too?

speaker: SS
words: Yes, kerosene. We had a little store up on the Ridge up there- he

speaker: SS
words: had a store and he carried all of his things mostly. He had a pack

speaker: SS
words: horse, and you know then those packboxes on the sides, and he would

speaker: SS
words: bring up coal oil and all that. And we'd get our coal oil there. And

speaker: SS
words: there mostly, was where we took our eggs and things, but he'd take the

speaker: SS
words: eggs down there- he'd gather them eggs up and Iguess he took 'em horse

speaker: SS
words: back, I don't remember any other way that he ever took 'em but horse

speaker: SS
words: back, and he'd bring those eggs down here then when we sold 'em to him

speaker: SS
words: up there. We'd getfdifferent little things we had to have, but we

speaker: SS
words: would put in quite a supply. Sometimes we'd send to Spokane and get

speaker: SS
words: a bill of some of the- like coffee and macaroni and all of those extra

speaker: SS
words: things- we'd send to Spokane for an order, and get that. And then the

speaker: SS
words: other stuff we would buy in Kendrick. That is, the biggest share of it

speaker: SS
words: But, we did send for awhile- we sent to Spokane. What was the name?

speaker: SS
words: Burgan's, I believe was the name of the store. In Spokane we sent for

speaker: SS
words: them.

speaker: SS
words: And they would deliver it by mail?

speaker: FVF
words: Yeah.

speaker: SS
words: Did you use the store on Cedar Creek very much?

speaker: FVF
words: Oh, yes, for all little things. And then once in awhile he'd get

speaker: FVF
words: maybe two or three bolts of calico or something like that, you know,

speaker: FVF
words: we'd get that there and little things. Like a sack of sugar,

speaker: FVF
words: we never thought of getting it up there. But then we did come down

speaker: FVF
words: here and get our sugar and flour and stuff down here; those bigger

speaker: FVF
words: things.

speaker: SS
words: Did you do much visiting when you came into Kendrick?

speaker: FVF
words: Oh, yes, we knew everybody in Kendrick. I can't say now who all the

speaker: FVF
words: people were. But this HwV * Long, we'd known him ever since I was

speaker: FVF
words: big enough to— well, him and Iwere about the same age, but his folks

speaker: FVF
words: were here and they had the store, and then Mwr-W carried it on. He's

speaker: FVF
words: been out of the store now for maybe five years. But he had carried it

speaker: FVF
words: on from his folks. They'd had it at Leland and then had it here and

speaker: FVF
words: he carried on til just a few years ago and then he had to give it up.

speaker: FVF
words: He's just a year younger than I.

speaker: SS
words: By the way, did your folks ever say to you, or give you an idea of

speaker: SS
words: why they finally picked around here to buy a place instead of somewhere

speaker: SS
words: else? What there was about Cedar Creek and Kendrick?

speaker: SS
words: Well, I don't know nothing in particular only they were over in Montana

speaker: SS
words: and they were in Kansas and those different places, you know, that

speaker: SS
words: sometimes they had a pretty hard time, that is, the people that lived

speaker: SS
words: there and as they come out here- we lived down here for, I guess, a

speaker: SS
words: year maybe before we went up there. They looked around and they thought

speaker: SS
words: was the best looking. And it happened to be that the people that

speaker: SS
words: we bought from, why, they was wanting to get out, and we got a chance

speaker: SS
words: to get this place. And they were looking for aplace and they finally

speaker: SS
words: decided that was it, and they liked everything, as far as I know, nothing

speaker: SS
words: in particular, only it was so much better than anyplace they'd

speaker: SS
words: ever been, that they figured that they liked it.

speaker: SS
words: Iguess part of it is that the crops are alot surer around here. I

speaker: SS
words: mean could count on it never had such a thing as a failure anywhere around here. And

speaker: SS
words: they were always - well, even today, the crops are just very good any

speaker: SS
words: where around. We had one wet year, oh, that's been a good many years

speaker: SS
words: ago, but we saved some of it. We saved enough to keep agoin' on. Not

speaker: SS
words: like some of 'em you know, was just wiped out of everything, but we

speaker: SS
words: never was. We never went hungry, I know that.

speaker: SS
words: Well, you know, you say that coming into to town just a couple times

speaker: SS
words: of year makes me think that it could be kind of lonely out there. Was

speaker: SS
words: it? Or did you have a lot of people?

speaker: FVF
words: Oh, no We made our own fun. As far as being lonely, we was never

speaker: FVF
words: lonely. We visited back and forth. We'd go and stay all night with

speaker: FVF
words: one family, all go over and stay with one, and then maybe the next

speaker: FVF
words: time the other family'd come over to your place, and we did like that.

speaker: FVF
words: And we had literary and dancing and popcorn- you know we'd all get

speaker: FVF
words: together and pop popcorn and things like that. No, we made our own-

speaker: FVF
words: Why, it was never known in those days; we used to come horseback- or

speaker: FVF
words: I didn't because I was too young, but my brother, my oldest brother,

speaker: FVF
words: he used to come over^Texas Ridge from Cedar Creek, he'd come over to

speaker: FVF
words: Texas Ridge to a dance or something like that. He'd come horseback.

speaker: FVF
words: But, we'd go with asled, if it was sledding time, we'd go with asled

speaker: FVF
words: from Cedar Creek over to Southwick in a sled. And that was a big deal

speaker: FVF
words: We'd take a big bobsled and everybody'd go together. And Christmas!

speaker: FVF
words: My, we had a big time Christmas tree and usually a program and a dance

speaker: FVF
words: and everything. We made our own entertainment. And then later years

speaker: FVF
words: different teachers came in there teachin'and they would have plays,

speaker: FVF
words: you know, different plays.

speaker: SS
words: Did you play party games?

speaker: FVF
words: Oh, yes! Yes, everything. All kinds of party games.

speaker: SS
words: Were there a lot of kids that were within a few years of your age?

speaker: FVF
words: Oh, yes, they was a bunch my age. Well, my mother and her friends,

speaker: FVF
words: was the ones that had all these parties and socials, you know, they

speaker: FVF
words: had these basket socials and hardtime parties. Everybody dress like

speaker: FVF
words: they didn't have anything or something, and oh, just entertainment.

speaker: FVF
words: We had entertainment, we never thought of goings I don't know when

speaker: FVF
words: it was, I don't remember how old I was before I ever saw picture show

speaker: FVF
words: or anything like that, like they have now. But, my, I thought the

speaker: FVF
words: first time- I don't know how old, I must have been in my twenties some

speaker: FVF
words: time. I had a baby or two and I went to Milton, Oregon to visit my

speaker: FVF
words: mother, she had moved down there, and I heard my first talking - the

speaker: FVF
words: lady that she worked for took us over to Walla Walla, and I thought

speaker: FVF
words: that was just something out of this world.

speaker: SS
words: It was a talking movie?

speaker: FVF
words: Yeah. I can remember so well (Chuckles) what I remember more than anything

speaker: FVF
words: else You've saw this picture, this funny picture, those two

speaker: FVF
words: birds, you know, that they're always around, they're crows, I think

speaker: FVF
words: they were, and they was all around everyplace, you know. And they was

speaker: FVF
words: following somebody, going with somebody, and the little one got so he

speaker: FVF
words: couldn't go, so he hops up on the other one! And I remember that just

speaker: FVF
words: as well as if it was yesterday. And I just thought that was something!

speaker: FVF
words: It was so cute, that I just thought it was just something out of this

speaker: FVF
words: world!

speaker: SS
words: Did they have actually in the film?

speaker: FVF
words: Oh, yeah.

speaker: SS
words: Talkie.

speaker: FVF
words: Yeah. It was the first one—^it was a story that always stayed in my

speaker: FVF
words: mind how easy it could be that people very innocently could get into

speaker: FVF
words: an awful mess and all of getting mixed up with somebody that got kil

speaker: FVF
words: led or something like that. And they tried to lay it onto somebody,

speaker: FVF
words: not the one that done it, but they tried to lay it onto them. And

speaker: FVF
words: this is what that picture was about. And it was so easy, that every

speaker: FVF
words: thing pointed to this one that they were trying to prosecute, but they

speaker: FVF
words: didn't get to prosectue him, it come out alright in the long run. But

speaker: FVF
words: that was the first one that I had saw. And it came out in this picture

speaker: SS
words: When you were growing up, what were the kind of things that you did

speaker: SS
words: that gave you enjoyment? Living in Cedar.

speaker: FVF
words: Oh, dancing and riding horses and chasing cattle and all that stuff!

speaker: FVF
words: (Chuckles) I liked to ride. I always had a horse, and I liked to

speaker: FVF
words: ride and do all that stuff. My two brothers, they went away to work

speaker: FVF
words: and I did quite a bit of the farm work, you know, plowing and taking

speaker: FVF
words: care of the horses. Iloved^ outside. I'd lots rather work outside

speaker: FVF
words: than I would inside when it come to work, I liked the outside work

speaker: FVF
words: and I love horses; always did. I always had ponies, good ponies to

speaker: FVF
words: ride.

speaker: SS
words: Were you still going to school when you were starting to help with the

speaker: SS
words: plowing and the farm work?

speaker: FVF
words: Yeah.

speaker: SS
words: Would you do that like after you'd get home from school? Or would you

speaker: SS
words: lay out of school?

speaker: FVF
words: Oh, Saturdays and Sundays i£ it was a busy time, and time, it

speaker: FVF
words: didn't make any difference.

speaker: SS
words: What were you using then? Were you using a footburner?

speaker: FVF
words: Yes. Horses.

speaker: SS
words: I've heard it's not the easiest work to do, using a footburner plow

speaker: SS
words: and getting it to go right.

speaker: FVF
words: No, but, if you're used to it, why, it's alright. The only thing I

speaker: FVF
words: couldn't do-after I was married I tried to run the binder, but I

speaker: FVF
words: couldn't make it work!! (Chuckles) Oh, I just had trouble with that.

speaker: FVF
words: But I could do most anything else. I could drive horses. I drove

speaker: FVF
words: horses, I could drive two or four or anything like that. But I didn't

speaker: FVF
words: make a go of the binder.

speaker: SS
words: So at that point, you must have been really useful to your mother for

speaker: SS
words: you to be able to do that kind of work.

speaker: FVF
words: Oh, yeah. Well, I just grew up with it. I just grew up with it and

speaker: FVF
words: that was it. Oh, I liked to farm.

words: working at that and his father was one of these traveling salesmen

words: that he drove and that's when they just had buggies and like that

words: you know to drive. And they drove all over the country and took or

speaker: SS
words: ders and brought in things. And like when they had a hotel here,

speaker: SS
words: they had a salesroom and he'd bring in his stuff there and show it

speaker: SS
words: in these different places where he'd go. And he was that, and sheand

speaker: SS
words: this boy, he was about twenty, I guess or twenty-one, and him

speaker: SS
words: and his brother was with the mother and^they had hired help and they

words: had aranch, and theyjraised cattle and numerous things like that.

words: But they come in- they had the money to just come in and buy what they

speaker: SS
words: wanted.

speaker: FVF
words: them that had- Well, the man died several years ago and the woman

speaker: FVF
words: up in the home at Moscow. A friend of ours that was raised right

speaker: FVF
words: there on the Ridge when Iwas ,K why, they lived right there all the

words: time, and so she just had to be- that is they put her in the home up

words: there. And she's been in there, but she was older than I was and

speaker: FVF
words: that's all it was. We just had the parties and^we finally decided to

words: get married, and so, that was it. My mother was a wid^ow then for a

words: Milton-Freewater and she went down there for a good many years. And

speaker: FVF
words: then my youngest brother lives at Spokane

words: that the kids nowdays- my gosh, they have to have it all before they

speaker: FVF

words: cook and do everything just better than Ican. But we all worked to

words: gether And whet I went outside to work Iwould plan what the girls

words: they was maybe six, eight years old. And every one of 'em can cook,

words: even my boys; every boy can cook, that I've got. And, oh, yes, then

words: we used to butcher our own meat and Imade my soap and we rendered

words: the lard. I've rendered just many gallons and gallons of lard. And

words: I sold the lard. Imade butter. Milked cows, made butter and I sold

words: cream, and all of that stuff went along with everything else. And

words: my canning- people won't believe when I tell 'em what I've cannedwhat

speaker: FVF
words: I used to can. I never thought of canning- after the children

speaker: FVF
words: got any size at all, my fruit was all 1in half gallon jars. A

speaker: FVF
words: quart, my, it wouldn't make ataste for 'em. So I'd have to can my

speaker: FVF
words: fruit in- and we would get six and seven hundred pounds of tomatoes

speaker: FVF
words: and peaches and prunes. Imade^prune butter and apple butter and stuff

speaker: FVF
words: like that, why, people'd make little jars of it now, Imade it by the

speaker: FVF
words: gallons. Just by the gallons. Well, we had to have it that way. The

speaker: FVF
words: children all liked it and we liked that, and we were , .eating that.

speaker: FVF
words: And then after I was on the farm my husband used to take our wh eatwe

speaker: FVF
words: took it down to and we'd get our winter's supply of Of in a week's

words: flour. And for many, many years Imade one sack of flour time in

words: bread. We ate that one sack of flour made up into bread in aweek's

speaker: FVF
words: time.

words: How much was a sack? Forty-nine pounds?

words: and all that stuff besides. The bread was the main thing that I had

speaker: FVF
words: to use that flour for.

speaker: SS
words: In a week's time?

speaker: FVF
words: In a week's time! Now, that's hard to belleye, but I did it for many,

speaker: FVF
words: many, months Not only weeks, but years, you might say. Iused a fif

speaker: FVF
words: ty pound sack of flour a week. And we would take our wheat down and

words: change it for flour down at There was amill down there. We

words: took it down there and we'd get our wheat and our oats and our diffe

words: rent stuff for mush, you know.

speaker: SS
words: Fifty pound sack' how much of that would go to your bread?

speaker: FVF
words: Well, the main part of it went to bread. Well, usually on Saturdays

speaker: FVF
words: I'd bake anywhere from- I won't say for sure, but I baked anywhere from

words: to ten to twelve loaves of bread on Saturday. I baked three times a

speaker: FVF
words: week, so you know what that took. It was no small amount. And then

speaker: FVF
words: we'd butcher a beef- it wasn't anything-^after the family got any size

words: at all, we butchered nine hogs at a time. And then cut it up, you

speaker: FVF
words: know and took care of it. Made it into hams and bacon. Well, I used

words: to can all the pigs' feet and headcheese and all that stuff, I made

words: that up, and then I'd have that to use along through the year. And we

words: mer. We'd smoke it you know, and then put it in sacks; hang it out.

speaker: FVF
words: sacks and hang it out there in this granary. It was cool in there all

words: the time. It was a big barn, great big barn, and there's where we hung

words: that meat and kept it all year.

speaker: FVF
words: Well, we didn't for a long— no, I canned that. Finally we got to

speaker: FVF
words: killing a beef- later years there was an old Dutchman that come from

speaker: FVF
words: Germany. He used to stay at our place quite a little bit and he was

speaker: FVF
words: a real butcher. And there was nothing x rent to waste. When he done

speaker: FVF
words: the butchering, there was nothing went to waste. He made up everything.

speaker: FVF

speaker: FVF
words: and shoulders and meat all down in the barrel and made the brine and

speaker: FVF
words: put over it. And he'd let it go so long- he had a homestead pretty

speaker: FVF
words: close to where we lived later years- and he'd come in and go back in

speaker: FVF
words: the cellar where we kept the barrels, and he'd go back in there and

speaker: FVF
words: he'd just swish his hand down in the barrel like that and come up and

speaker: FVF
words: it was alright or he had to take it out and hang it up and let it

words: the meat, drain and he rehet that stuff and then put it back on~ We did that

words: made liverwurst andKblood sausage and all kinds of stuffed sausage and

words: we had that, too. So, we had everything in the meat line to eat.

words: He would be able to tell from running his hand through the brine, and

words: he could tell?

speaker: FVF
words: Yeah. He'd just come right in and just swish his hand down, that's

speaker: FVF
words: the way he'd do. He'd swish his hand right down in the barrel like

speaker: FVF
words: that and feel of it and there was a feeling or something, and then if

speaker: FVF
words: it had to be taken out, why, he'd just took it all out and hung it

speaker: FVF
words: up in the smokehousef |eV it drain while he got this- would

speaker: FVF
words: reheat this, you know, and get it ready.

words: he didn't, but for later years, he had a little homestead, and he'd

words: ly said that; of that stuff. Like how many cans of peaches and toma

words: toes.

words: pounds, I'd buy.

words: there maybe one day and get theshing and maybe it'd rain, and I had

speaker: FVF
words: the birds for their eats and

words: woulda had to went quite a ways to went home. And these people were

words: cold in the fall with the beans, and they wouldn't even let 'em in

words: the house, hardly to wash or anything. Our house was just a house they

words: could come to and come in. We had an extra big long room there, and

words: then they'd come in there and set by this big fire; big stove, big pot

words: With the neighbors?

words: With the neighbors and like that, or whoever happened to be there. If

words: the threshing crew was there.

words: than what the kids would think it would be today, was good times just

words: visiting then?

words: visit and then have a big meal. Dinners, lots of times- maybe this

words: year they'd be a bunch come to our place and it wasn't anything for us

words: to have- 'course, there was quite a few of our own family, and neigh

words: bors, thirty or forty was no job at all. Just had a big dinner.

words: At your place?

speaker: FVF
words: it'd be according to what the weather was, and we'd go over and have

words: one time we was over there and had a big Easter dinner and we had our

speaker: FVF
words: Easter dinner down in the lower part and then we had tobggans and stuff

words: and there was a hill there that we could come down, you know, toboggan

words: on the hill; the snow was crusted and we could ride on that. That was

words: at Eastertime one year. Course, it wasn't every year. Just things

words: like that. We hadAsleigh parties and stuff like that, too.

words: Oh, no, we had 'em every once in a while. We'd go to one house and

speaker: FVF
words: then to another. We'd go for a surprise on somebody maybe for an even

words: ing of dancing and like that. And we'd go and maybe sometimes try

words: to fool 'em, just come on 'em unexpected and things like that. And

words: then go home. Things like that.

words: and whatever they happened to have. Harp; mouthorgan , we danced to

speaker: FVF
words: Oh, violin-

speaker: SS
words: I mean, the kind of stuff- the kind of songs, was it the oldfashioned?

speaker: FVF

speaker: SS
words: visiting?

speaker: FVF
words: Oh, yes! We'd take our kids. And we had a place fix especially in our

speaker: FVF
words: hall, at the schoolhouse, upstairs to the schoolhouse, that we had

words: for the dancehall, and we had a place there and put our babies down

speaker: FVF
words: Yes. (interrupted by telephone) Bad times too, but then nothing so

speaker: FVF
words: bad that— Each neighbor helped the other one. If somebody was sick

speaker: FVF
words: why my mother used to go and help the neighbors a great deal. And

speaker: FVF
words: she would be called out anytime, day or night, she would go and help

speaker: FVF
words: the neighbors. I helped a lot of my neighbors when I was up there,

words: too. We just kind of depended on one another. That was the way it

speaker: FVF
words: went.

words: What were-some of the bad times that you're thinking of?

speaker: FVF
words: Oh, nothing in particular; only sickness or something like that. But

speaker: FVF
words: we never thought of a doctor. It was just do it yourself. You had to

speaker: FVF
words: do what there was. If you thought you was doing right, that was it.

speaker: FVF
words: It's very seldom that we ever went to a doctor. We'd "ttlmik we had,

speaker: FVF
words: this or that or something else.

words: What could you do in the case of sickness? If you went to somebody's

words: house to try to help them? What could you do? You didn't have any

words: medicine of the type that the doctors would prescribe.

speaker: FVF
words: No, that the doctors would give, but we'd have— each household had

speaker: FVF
words: different things that they used. Some would use one thing and some

words: would use something else, andAwe'd do the best we could with what we

speaker: FVF
words: had, and just pass it out to whoever was sick. But sometimes it was

words: just colds and sometimes it was the mother that was sick and sometimes

words: it was someone else that she needed help, and like that, and we just

words: helped one another.

words: it, but at my time that I was up there, I don't I know of anybody

words: that had it. But before I was around very much, there was one or tw

words: that passed away on our ridge of tuberculosis. Some of the girls—

speaker: FVF
words: she's still alive there's a lady now, she's in her eighties, she was a neighbor and

speaker: FVF
words: her

speaker: FVF
words: mother died with it- her mother died with the childbirth. And then

speaker: FVF
words: she was the oldest one in the family- that is the oldest girl, and

words: then there was a brother older than she was. And the grandfather had

words: died with tuberculosis and this boy took it and he died with it- tub

speaker: FVF
words: erculosis. Well, that was the time that we were younger people up

words: there, because she was taking care of the family. And then everybody'd

words: go and help if they'd get down and were sick and had to have someone

words: to be with 'em at night or something like that. People would just take

words: turns and go and stay; each neighbor would. flVtybLpd go a night and

words: they'd go a night and somebody else'd go some other night. In those

words: And there was usually one or two that could take care of the bodies.

words: And we'd set up at night, you know, somebody'd stay there all night,

words: and then we'd bury 'em!

words: was some people up there that made the boxes and caskets and every

words: thing; fixed it, just as nice as anything. But there was people in

words: the neighborhood that did that work you know, would do those things.

speaker: FVF
words: Then there was people that would- well, we had a lady there that'd

words: always go when there was babies come, they'd always go to anybody's

words: house that was there and needed it, why, they'd always go and help

words: with them. For years and years they never had a doctor, just one of

speaker: FVF
words: ing everybody. And he was one of these fellows- Well, the neighbors

speaker: FVF
words: up there, if you happened to be walking to town or walking someplace

speaker: FVF
words: or anything, they'd pick you up. When they had a car, that is a pickup

speaker: FVF
words: or or something, they always'd ask the neighbors to ride with

speaker: FVF
words: 'em. Well, one time these boys was coming down the road and this fel

speaker: FVF
words: low was walking; no, they picked him up, he was on his way home, and

speaker: FVF
words: they picked him up, and when he went to get out of the pickup or rig,

speaker: FVF
words: when he went to get out, he stumbled and he didn't fall, but he stum

speaker: FVF
words: bled, and he turned around and he says, "Well, it's a good thing I

speaker: FVF
words: didn't fall and hurt myself," he said, "I'd a sued you." Now that's

speaker: FVF
words: the kind of fellow he was. So, we kinda commenced to kinda steer around

speaker: FVF
words: 'em. But that's the only one. There was people up there, quite

speaker: FVF
words: a few people up there that lived- didn't have too much, but maybe if

speaker: FVF
words: I went over someplace and I had an extra supply of something, I'd take

speaker: FVF
words: 'em something. Well, if they had an extra supply of something they'd

speaker: FVF
words: bring me something or they'd take something to somebody else. We al

speaker: FVF
words: ways had a little extra that we could divide with our neighbors. We

speaker: FVF
words: never was so hard up but that we could- you know, could each have a

speaker: FVF
words: little something. And that's the way we did.

speaker: SS
words: When something like that- did you try to figure what your neighbor

speaker: SS
words: might need? Or did they have to say to you what they needed?

speaker: FVF
words: No. They was neighbors that .ask you, or even let you know. They

speaker: FVF
words: didn't want you to really know. But then, you could tell, when you'd

speaker: FVF
words: go, we'd visit one another all the time and you could tell if they

speaker: FVF
words: was out of this or they was out of that or they were out of something

speaker: FVF
words: else. And then maybe next time when we went, why, we took- if we had

speaker: FVF
words: a supply- we took it. And that's the way they all did. Each one hel

speaker: FVF
words: ped the other.

speaker: SS
words: It wasn't something that anybody would keep track of? What jou owed

speaker: SS
words: somebody or anything like that?

speaker: FVF
words: No.

speaker: SS
words: Or wasn't something that you did this year and it could be years from

speaker: SS
words: now that they might help you.

speaker: FVF
words: Well, but then, we were all kinda in the same boat. So they would all

speaker: FVF
words: do the same thing. If we needed something, well, we'd get it.. If any

speaker: FVF
words: of them found it out, that is, we'd get helped on it, and things like

speaker: FVF
words: that. Just like I started to tell you about this man that used to come

speaker: FVF
words: to my mother's there to help her when Dad was gone all the time; and

speaker: FVF
words: he'a come over there and help her, and at the end of the year, every

speaker: FVF
words: year, didn't make any difference whether they had a dime or didn't

speaker: FVF
words: have a dime, they'd always- my dad and him, would always figure outthey

speaker: FVF
words: put down everything through the years- if he worked and did some

speaker: FVF
words: thing special, my dad was supposed to pay him for it, why then, if

speaker: FVF
words: Dad got him something, why, Dad would pay for it, and then at the end

speaker: FVF
words: of the year, why, they would always straighten up their books. They

speaker: FVF
words: would always get their books straight. Sometimes they'd owe one, some

speaker: FVF
words: times they'd owe the other. But they had these books straight every

speaker: FVF
words: year.

speaker: SS
words: He actually put in the crop for your father?

speaker: FVF
words: Yeah.

speaker: SS
words: And he would harvest it, too.

speaker: FVF
words: Yeah.

speaker: SS
words: And your father would pay him for the work?

speaker: FVF
words: And one year they logged a little bit, and oh, just different little

speaker: FVF
words: things like that that he done that my dad couldn't do or didn't do,

speaker: FVF
words: but they'd always have their books and they'd put it down. They knew

speaker: FVF
words: just what there was, and then when it come to— 'Cause my dad didn't

speaker: FVF
words: know anything about farming when he went up there on Cedar Creek.

speaker: FVF
words: Talk about the singletrees and doubletrees and all of that stuff, you

speaker: FVF
words: know, and this fell, had what they call an evener- well, it's a long

speaker: FVF
words: piece of timber- maybe you know what they are.

speaker: SS
words: I think I do, but-

speaker: FVF
words: Well, there's a short end and a long end; well, they put the one

speaker: FVF
words: horse on the long end and put two horses on the short end. That's

speaker: FVF
words: what they call an evener. And these fellows, they didn't know why

speaker: FVF
words: this one old horse couldn't keep up, cause they put her on the -

speaker: FVF
words: either end and she was working against the other two! (Chuckles) We

speaker: FVF
words: used to laugh about that.

speaker: SS
words: Who did that? Was that your father?

speaker: FVF
words: My dad.

speaker: SS
words: Did he learn eventually?

speaker: FVF
words: Oh, yes, he did. Then he used to, in the early days, too, you know,

speaker: FVF
words: it was hard to buy shells and all that stuff for your gun and there

speaker: FVF
words: was lots of birds. In the fall there was lots of young birds, you

speaker: FVF
words: know. Well, my dad always would pick up little rocks, about that big,

speaker: FVF
words: he'd always have his pocket full of rocks, and these birds'd fly up

speaker: FVF
words: in the tree and he could pick a bird out of a tree with a rock, just

speaker: FVF
words: easier than you could shoot, almost! That way he saved his .22 shells

speaker: FVF
words: and instead of using them for birds, he could use that for something

speaker: FVF
words: else. And he'd have these rocks and he could knock 'em out of there.

speaker: SS
words: Were those grouse and pheasants?

speaker: FVF
words: Grouse and pheasants.

speaker: SS
words: That sounds pretty slick to me.

speaker: FVF
words: Well, it was slick! And at that time they was so plentiful that they

speaker: FVF
words: weren't scared to death like they are now. You can't hardly get near

speaker: FVF
words: one now, you know, because, well, there's so few of 'em that you can't

speaker: FVF
words: hardly get near 'em. But in those days, they'd hop up there and be up

speaker: FVF
words: there in the trees and we'd all be looking to see tfiere they were, you

speaker: FVF
words: know, and then finally we'd locate them. And then, my boys got to

speaker: FVF
words: doing that. One of my boys could throw a rock pretty good. He was

speaker: FVF
words: a pretty good shot.

speaker: SS
words: Well, was there a church at Cedar Creek then? Any kind of church

speaker: SS
words: that you had?

speaker: FVF
words: No, not for a long, long time, there was no church there. They would

speaker: FVF
words: have services maybe at somebody's house or something like that. There's

speaker: FVF
words: a church up there now and it's been there for quite a while. They'd

speaker: FVF
words: have a church at the schoolhouse, but they didn't have a regular church.

speaker: FVF
words: And when they did it was nondemoninational. Anybody could go, and

speaker: FVF
words: everybody was welcome to come whoever come in there to preach

speaker: FVF
words: a sermon, or anything like that. I know that we had a quite a fight

speaker: FVF
words: after we got the church up there. Why, there was quite a fight went on

speaker: FVF
words: in the neighborhood about having dances. And some people were just so_

speaker: FVF
words: against that, and all like that. And some of the ministers were against

speaker: FVF
words: it too, but there was one minister that they all pretty well liked,

speaker: FVF
words: real good. And they would always have a harvest ball, after the har

speaker: FVF
words: vest was all over, well, we'd have a great big dance. And we'd have

speaker: FVF
words: Christmas, maybe a Christmas ball, or something like that. Well, one

speaker: FVF
words: time this fellow come over and he was to preach, and they were having

speaker: FVF
words: what they called protracted meetings- you know what they were- used to

speaker: FVF
words: go out and stay for weeks. And that one fellow said, somebody said to

speaker: FVF
words: him, how long was it going to last, and he said, well, he didn't know

speaker: FVF
words: I guess, as long as the eats was left! (Chuckles) And things like that.

speaker: FVF
words: This minister said to the boys, "Now," he said, - he knew who the boys

speaker: FVF
words: were that were giving- a certain number would give a dance one time,

speaker: FVF
words: ther time.someone else would do it-. He w ent to these

speaker: FVF
words: boys, and he says, "Now, boys, I'll tell you what I'll do." He says,

speaker: FVF
words: "If you'll all come over and listen to my preachin'," he says, "I'll

speaker: FVF
words: start a half hour or an early and you all come over and listen to me."

speaker: FVF
words: And then, he says, "You can go over and have your dance." And he says,

speaker: FVF
words: "if I wasn't a minister, if I wasn't supposed to be a minister, I

speaker: FVF
words: wouldn't mind going over too, but," he says, "that isn't the way I

speaker: FVF
words: can do. But I'll have my meetings." And, you know, there was every

speaker: FVF
words: body went to that church, and when it was over, why, we went over and

speaker: FVF
words: had our dance.

speaker: SS
words: The harvest ball?

speaker: FVF
words: Well, I don't know whether it was the harvest ball, but they were ha

speaker: FVF
words: ving those protracted meetings anyway at the time.

speaker: SS
words: Well it's kind of funny that some people would get opposed to dancing

speaker: SS
words: if it had been going on in the neighborhood for so long. From what

speaker: SS
words: you say, they'd always had dances in the neighborhood.

speaker: FVF
words: Well, we always had dances, but then they had their church, too, but

speaker: FVF
words: then they didn't all go to church, I know that.

speaker: SS
words: But this not liking dancing kind of grew when they built the church.

speaker: FVF
words: Yes, they tried every way they could to keep them from dancing. But

speaker: FVF
words: then they didn't. The neighborhoods- they'd be from Southwick and -Wc^

speaker: FVF
words: what we called Boulder Creek and all around there, why, they'd be peo

speaker: FVF
words: ple come for miles to our dances up there. We had big dances, every

speaker: FVF
words: body'd come.

speaker: SS
words: And those would be in the school, mostly?

speaker: FVF
words: At night. Yeah. And things like that.

speaker: SS
words: You had a second floor, you say, in the school for dancing?

speaker: FVF
words: Yeah, they had the upstairs for dancing. And it still stands up there,

speaker: FVF
words: the schoolhouse, and they're using the lower part of it, they kind of

speaker: FVF
words: fixed it up, and we have our pioneer picnic the last Sunday of this

speaker: FVF
words: month. We have a pioneer picnic and we've been wanting to get that

speaker: FVF
words: fixed so they can have it up there. But they haven't had it. They

speaker: FVF
words: had it down at Clarkston for many years, and last year, or two years,

speaker: FVF
words: they've had it over at here at the Fire Hall. And all the old pio

speaker: FVF
words: neers that's around are supposed to come. So that's come up now. I

speaker: FVF
words: don't know for sure whether they'll have it this year or not.

speaker: SS
words: Is Cedar Creek what they called the neighborhood? Is that what they

speaker: SS
words: called the neighborhood?

speaker: FVF
words: Yeah. The Cedar Creek neighborhood. Then there was Park and there

speaker: FVF
words: was a Crescent and Southwick and all those little places around there.

speaker: FVF
words: But we all congregated together. We've had lots of people c ome from

speaker: FVF
words: those other places, too. And they went, a lot of people went to them.

speaker: FVF
words: Lot of times these fellows'd play for our dances up there'd play for

speaker: FVF
words: these others dances, too.

speaker: SS
words: What I call revivals? Is that what they were?

speaker: FVF
words: Well, some of these was that have their tents; have their big

speaker: FVF
words: tent and then everybody goes and maybe stays nights, and they have it

speaker: FVF
words: for a week or so. And they'll take their- used to- go and have these

speaker: FVF
words: protracted meetings, or revivals, or whatever, I don't know what they

speaker: FVF
words: call em now. But they used to always go and then people'd go and

speaker: FVF
words: come- them that was close enough, they'd go and come, but they always

speaker: FVF
words: took eats, you know. Everybody donated the eats. And every

speaker: FVF
words: body 'd go and take all their kids and everything. That was a big

speaker: FVF
words: to-do when they had them.

speaker: SS
words: Where would they set up their tents?

speaker: FVF
words: Well, right out in the woods.

speaker: SS
words: Right on Cedar Creek?

speaker: FVF
words: Well, somewhere around there. They usually had it between Cedar Creek

speaker: FVF
words: and Southwick, is where they used to have it all the time.

speaker: SS
words: So people'd come from both places.

speaker: FVF
words: All places.

speaker: SS
words: Would there be usually the same denomination that would put these on?

speaker: FVF
words: Well, I don't think so. I don't remember, they just had the meetings

speaker: FVF
words: and everybody that wanted to- because afterwards- I think they just go

speaker: FVF
words: and give theirselves to Christ, and'they would have a big day of bap^

speaker: FVF
words: tising and stuff like that afterwards.

speaker: SS
words: Baptise them right in the creek?

speaker: FVF
words: Yeah, they was a creek not too far from where they had it there. They'd

speaker: FVF
words: go up there and have it. There was quite a little hole there that they

speaker: FVF
words: had that they baptised 'em in.

speaker: SS
words: People have said to me that that stuff was pretty emotional for a lot

speaker: SS
words: of people that would go.

speaker: FVF
words: Oh, yes, some of 'em'd just go crazy! Just go crazy! But people do

speaker: FVF
words: that anyway! I know some of 'em'd just go wild. Then we used to have

speaker: FVF
words: what we called chautauquas. Did you ever—?

speaker: SS
words: I've heard of them.

speaker: FVF
words: Used to go to them. They had 'em here in town.

speaker: SS
words: Kendrick?

speaker: FVF
words: Uh-huh. Used to come down.

speaker: SS
words: Were they good?

speaker: FVF
words: Yeah. They had/Nsinging- mostly singing^was all. But everybody'd aome

speaker: FVF
words: and bring their kids. And they had a big tent down here someplace.

speaker: FVF
words: One night we come down here, it was in the summertime, and the dust

speaker: FVF
words: was about that deep. We come down here and it come up a shower and

speaker: FVF
words: there wasn't any of us could get out, we had to stay in town that night!

speaker: FVF
words: (Chuckles) Just come enough rain that it just wet that just enough

speaker: FVF
words: that we couldn't get out. We had trucks.

speaker: SS
words: Too slick.

speaker: FVF
words: Uh-huh. Tod slick, we couldn't get out.

speaker: SS
words: Where did people stay? At other people's houses?

speaker: FVF
words: Well, they all* 'em had friends. And then there was the hotel there,

speaker: FVF
words: up here. But that hotel.has saw it's last days, as far as most people

speaker: FVF
words: are concerned. Those people that's running this farm down below here,

speaker: FVF
words: the ones that has this Country Kitchen, well, they're the ones that

speaker: FVF
words: got the hotel and.had quite a stir over some stuff that they had there.

speaker: FVF
words: Well, they just don't keep things clean, that's all there is to it. I

speaker: FVF
words: don't know what'11 happen. But I just thought last night, of all

speaker: FVF
words: things, if people come in here, happen to got caught here in town, I

speaker: FVF
words: don't know where they'd stay, because I wouldn't want to see anybody

speaker: FVF
words: go stay there at the hotel.

speaker: SS
words: Because it isn't sanitary anymore?

speaker: FVF
words: I don't think it is. I don't think there's anything sanitary there.

speaker: FVF
words: And they got this scabies or some of that stuff in there! I wouldn't

speaker: FVF
words: want to have to stay there. And, of course, people that'd come in

speaker: FVF
words: wouldn't know it.

speaker: SS
words: There's one thing about those protracted meetings that I was going to

speaker: SS
words: ask you about; and that is, people have said to me that they thought

speaker: SS
words: the effects of that wouldn't be too lasting a lot of times. That peo

speaker: SS
words: ple might be converted and then they would backslide again. Do you

speaker: SS
words: think that would be true, or do you think they really changed the, you

speaker: SS
words: know, the spiritual outlook?

speaker: FVF
words: Well, I think it did for some people. But then you know, some p eople

speaker: FVF
words: are just that way. Now just like my brother; they weren't- they weren't

speaker: FVF
words: much on the church deal and they went down- they were down Hood Ri

speaker: FVF
words: ver, and he always followed the harvesting. He'd start in down there

speaker: FVF
words: and then he'd harvest clear through up to Idaho. And so, one year they

speaker: FVF
words: came up, and he was taking the family home and then he was going to

speaker: FVF
words: come back and I was visiting my mother down at Milton-Freewater. So

speaker: FVF
words: he wanted me to go back with him and then he'd- then when he come back

speaker: FVF
words: I could go down there just for the trip and then back with him. He

speaker: FVF
words: was coming right back; so I did. And him and her had just gone crazy

speaker: FVF
words: over this- oh, it wasn't Billy Graham, but another fella—

speaker: SS
words: Billy Sunday?

speaker: FVF
words: Yes, I guess it was Billy Sunday.

speaker: SS
words: He was pretty well known in those days.

speaker: FVF
words: Yes, It was Billy Sunday. So they wanted me to go with 'em, they were

speaker: FVF
words: going to church and they wanted me to go with 'em, so I went with 'em

speaker: FVF
words: one night. And, now, they had a family and they lived just right on

speaker: FVF
words: a street there, and they left those children home while they went to

speaker: FVF
words: this meeting and I went with 'em. And I never was in such a place in

speaker: FVF
words: my life! (Chuckles) So, when we got home, they was talkin' about

speaker: FVF
words: Brother so-and-so and this and that and the other and they thought he

speaker: FVF
words: was just- maybe he was! I don't know, but to me- I said, "Well, I'll

speaker: FVF
words: tell you one thing, if I had any young folks, any young boys or girls,

speaker: FVF
words: they wouldn't step in that place if I knew it." Because he got up

speaker: FVF
words: there in front and he just went plumb crazy! Now that's all there is

speaker: FVF
words: to it! He undone his tie and he throwed it on the floor and he just

speaker: FVF
words: took off everything one at a time, he was worked up, you know, he was

speaker: FVF
words: so worked up and all, and he just kept doing that. And I said, "I

speaker: FVF
words: would never allow my children to go to anything like that." Because,

speaker: FVF
words: as you say, I think it just worked on their- just get 'em in such a

speaker: FVF
words: state that they didn't know what they was doing.

speaker: SS
words: Did they bring their kids to it?

speaker: FVF
words: No. They didn't take their kids. And I said, "Well, I think your

speaker: FVF
words: first duty is at home. And if you let them kids run wild, they way

speaker: FVF
words: you do, and go there. I don't think that's what you should do." I

speaker: FVF
words: said, "Your first duty is there at home with them kids."

speaker: SS
words: How do you think it worked on Cedar Ridge as far as— there wasn't any

speaker: SS
words: one denomination or anything; do you think that that hurt at all, any

speaker: SS
words: body, or do you think it's just as well to have a community- if you're

speaker: SS
words: going to have a church, to have a community church like that?

speaker: FVF
words: Well, I just really don't know. But there was only a few people in

speaker: FVF
words: that part of the country that went to most of these places. That ever

speaker: FVF
words: got in such a pitch as that, just all worked up. But I don't believe

speaker: FVF
words: in that. I don t think you have to get and make a big to-do or

speaker: FVF
words: anything. I don't know how other people believe, or anything, but I

speaker: FVF
words: think that - in my estimation, it shows- well, I don't know what you

speaker: FVF
words: would call it- but anyway, just like I said to him, I said, "I don't

speaker: FVF
words: believe in that." Now, that's all there is to it. That emotion, may

speaker: FVF
words: for the time being, it might be alright, but when they're over that

speaker: FVF
words: why then, that's all there is to it. And I don't believe that anyone

speaker: FVF
words: can go to a place like that and go forward and give themselves to the

speaker: FVF
words: Lord and all like that and then go home and be a regular old hypocrite.

speaker: FVF
words: Now, that's the way I , but then, everybody's different. They have

speaker: FVF
words: their own ways.

speaker: SS
words: If you have some kind of religious feeling at all, it ought to express

speaker: SS
words: itself in your life and the way you act towards other people.

speaker: FVF
words: That's what I say. Well, I've saw one or two people; we had one or two

speaker: FVF
words: neighbors up there on our Ridge that I said if there ever was Christians

speaker: FVF
words: those two women were Christians. I don't care how people would talk,

speaker: FVF
words: you know they'll get to talking about somebody doing this and somebody

speaker: FVF
words: doing that and they're doing, this and that and the other, and those

speaker: FVF
words: two people could always see something good in those ones that they were

speaker: FVF
words: talking about, thought they were so terrible. And you know, you

speaker: FVF
words: look around and if you stop to think there's just most anyone- there's

speaker: FVF
words: something good in 'em if you happen to hit the right chord. There's

speaker: FVF
words: something good in everybody.

speaker: SS
words: So these women didn't judge other people?

speaker: FVF
words: No. But they would always say, if they were talking about this party,

speaker: FVF
words: or anything, they'd always say something good about thi s person. They

speaker: FVF
words: would do this or they would do that. For instance, there was one fel

speaker: FVF
words: la up there that, oh, he was a kind of a harem-skarem, you know. But

speaker: FVF
words: he was the best person that you ever saw, if you needed someone or you

speaker: FVF
words: needed somebody to go to town get something, or if you had to have

speaker: FVF
words: someone -to stay with the sick, or if you had to have something done,

speaker: FVF
words: like someone passed away or something, and they had to be shaved and

speaker: FVF
words: dressed and taken care of, he'd do that. He'd just do anything like

speaker: FVF
words: that, and he had this other- he wasn't a very good character, as far

speaker: FVF
words: as that was concerned. He'd do all these things.

speaker: SS
words: When you say haren-skarem, like would he drink a lot?

speaker: FVF
words: Well, yes, to a certain extent. Just anything. But he was good in-

speaker: SS
words: He had a wild streak.

speaker: FVF
words: Yeah, just a wild streak, that people thought was terrible.

speaker: SS
words: But all these things that he did. When they started a church there

speaker: SS
words: was it a community church. When they built the church.

speaker: FVF
words: Well, it was supposed to be a United Brethern, I think was

speaker: FVF
words: the name of it. But then any denomination, if they could get anyone

speaker: FVF
words: to come there to preach, why, it was any denomination that would come.

speaker: FVF
words: But it's just worked out til the old-timers are just all gone and they

speaker: FVF
words: don't use it any more. They did have Sunday School for many years,

speaker: FVF
words: but J don't think they have Sunday School anymore. Don't think there's

speaker: FVF
words: anyanybody'd come. I went up there a time or two and stayed with my

speaker: FVF
words: neighbor and we went . on Sunday and there wasn't anybody come,

speaker: FVF
words: but just her and I and another neighbor - he was supposed to be the

speaker: FVF
words: main one. And now, he's away from up there and she's gone, the lady's

speaker: FVF
words: gone. So I don't think they have any gatherings at the church anymore.
